prestazioni: Engine swap to Nissan HR15DE (1.5L DOHC 16V) with a HKS GT-SS turbo kit (Garrett GT2560R-5 turbocharger, HKS 264° camshafts, HKS Hi-Power exhaust manifold, and HKS SSQV blow-off valve) paired with a Haltech Elite 1500 ECU for precise fuel and ignition mapping, targeting 220 HP at 7,000 RPM with 98 RON fuel

KW Suspension Variant 3 coilovers (rebound-adjustable dampers, 30mm drop, and pillow-ball top mounts) with Eibach Pro-Kit springs for optimized handling, paired with Cusco 22mm front and 19mm rear sway bars to reduce body roll in tight corners

Nismo 4-piston front brake calipers (from the Nissan March Super Turbo) with DBA 5000 Series rotors (320mm front, 280mm rear) and Project Mu 999 brake pads, paired with stainless steel braided brake lines and Motul RBF 660 brake fluid for consistent pedal feel
estetica: Veilside Fortune 03 widebody kit (modified to fit the Clipper’s unique chassis dimensions) with custom carbon fiber overfenders, paired with a custom 3-piece Work Emotion CR Kiwami wheels (15x7 +35 front, 15x8 +30 rear) in gloss black with gold accents

Seibon carbon fiber hood (custom-made for the Clipper’s bolt pattern) with a functional NACA duct for engine bay cooling, paired with a custom rear diffuser from Rocket Bunny (modified to fit the Clipper’s rear bumper) and a Cusco rear spoiler for downforce

Full interior overhaul with Bride Euro X-10 bucket seats (custom-molded to the Clipper’s floorpan), a Nardi Classic 350mm steering wheel, and a custom roll cage from AutoPower Industries (4-point bolt-in, TIG-welded) with red powder coating and matching red Alcantara door cards
